title: openai optional-peer preflight warning and missing-package error
description: Verify the existing OpenAI missing-package error fires when the openai package is absent, and add a preflight check that prints an actionable hint (npm install openai) when a DeepSeek or OpenAI provider is configured in bober.config.json but the openai package is not installed, before the first model call.

successCriteria.description: A unit test asserts that OpenAIAdapter.chat (or getClient) throws an error whose message contains 'npm install openai' when import("openai") is mocked to reject.

A preflight function, given a config whose any role resolves to provider openai, openai-compat, or a deepseek shorthand, and with the openai package mocked as not-installed, returns or emits a hint string that contains 'npm install openai'.
The same preflight function, when the openai package is mocked as installed (import resolves), returns no hint / emits nothing for the same config — asserted by a unit test.
The preflight function, given a config that uses only anthropic across all roles, returns no hint even when openai is absent — asserted by a unit test.
npm run build exits 0 and npm run lint exits 0 after the changes.

nonGoals: Do not make the missing-openai preflight a hard error that aborts startup — it must be a warning/hint (users may install openai later).
Do not auto-install the openai package on the user's behalf.
Do not change the existing call-time hard error wording in openai.ts beyond verifying it.
Do not add openai to package.json dependencies.
stopConditions: The missing-package error is asserted by a test, the preflight emits the install hint only when an openai-family provider is configured and openai is absent, and build plus lint exit 0.
definitionOfDone: A user who sets a DeepSeek or OpenAI provider in bober.config.json without having run npm install openai sees an actionable hint naming 'npm install openai' before the first model call, instead of a deep stack trace. A user on anthropic-only config sees nothing. If the package is present, the preflight is silent. The pre-existing call-time error in the openai adapter remains as the backstop.
assumptions: Q3: the openai-absent preflight is a warning/hint, not a hard error — evidence: PRD US-004 says 'prints an actionable hint', Open Questions leans warning.
Detecting openai presence is done by attempting a dynamic import and catching rejection, mirroring openai.ts:280-287 — evidence: that file's getClient pattern.

Roles that resolve to an openai-family provider are detected via resolveProviderModel over each role's model/provider in config — evidence: resolveProviderModel in model-resolver.ts and per-role provider fields in schema.ts.
generatorNotes: The missing-package throw already exists in src/providers/openai.ts (line ~284-286: 'OpenAI provider requires the "openai" package. Run: npm install openai'). Add a test that mocks import("openai") to reject and asserts the throw. For the preflight, add a function (e.g. preflightOpenaiPeer(config) in a new src/providers/preflight.ts or in factory.ts) that: (1) scans config roles (planner/curator/generator/evaluator/codeReview and researcher) resolving each via resolveProviderModel(model, provider) to see if any is openai/openai-compat (including deepseek shorthands which resolve to openai-compat), (2) attempts import("openai") and on rejection returns/logs a hint containing 'npm install openai'. Use logger from src/utils/logger.ts for emission if logging. Mock the dynamic import in tests.
evaluatorNotes: Run npm test. Confirm one test asserts the openai adapter's missing-package error contains 'npm install openai' (import mocked to reject). Confirm preflight tests cover: openai-family provider configured + openai absent => hint with 'npm install openai'; openai present => no hint; anthropic-only => no hint. Run npm run build and npm run lint, assert exit 0.
